condition.name: Neonatal Cholestasis
overview: Neonatal cholestasis is conjugated hyperbilirubinemia in the first months of life.
Early recognition and referral are critical to prevent progressive liver damage, biliary cirrhosis and death.
Cholestasis is often mistaken for prolonged physiological jaundice, so any prolonged jaundice must be fractionated.

iap_espghan_criteria: Direct (conjugated) bilirubin >1 mg/dL if total bilirubin is <5 mg/dL
Direct bilirubin >20% of total bilirubin if total bilirubin is >5 mg/dL
definition.screening_trigger: Evaluation should begin if jaundice persists beyond 14 days in term infants.

history_template: Whether jaundice is persisting beyond 14 days
Color of stool (pale, clay, white or acholic)
Family history including sibling deaths or liver disease
History suggestive of IUGR or syndromic disorder
examination_template: Dysmorphic features such as features of Alagille syndrome
red_flags: Sibling deaths due to liver disease

first_line_investigations.name: Liver Function Tests
first_line_investigations.purpose: Confirm cholestasis and assess hepatocellular injury

first_line_investigations.name: PT / INR
first_line_investigations.purpose: Assess coagulation status and vitamin K related malabsorption

first_line_investigations.name: GGT and ALP
first_line_investigations.purpose: Clues to biliary versus hepatocellular origin

first_line_investigations.name: Abdominal USG
first_line_investigations.purpose: Rule out choledochal cyst or biliary atresia and assess gallbladder

first_line_investigations.name: Stool color card
first_line_investigations.purpose: Visual parent-based recognition of pale stools

additional_workup_if_indicated: Liver biopsy if advised by specialist

clinical_clues_summary.clue_toward: Obstructive jaundice such as biliary atresia
Syndromic disorder such as Alagille syndrome

immediate_actions: Measure direct and total bilirubin urgently
Order LFTs, PT/INR and abdominal ultrasound
Refer early to pediatric gastroenterologist / hepatologist if biliary atresia suspected

action: Ultrasound and HIDA scan as advised
Early Kasai procedure ideally before 45-60 days

parent_counseling: Not all jaundice is benign after 2 weeks of age. — 2 हफ्ते बाद भी पीलिया सामान्य नहीं माना जाना चाहिए।
Pale stools and dark urine are danger signs and need urgent evaluation. — सफ़ेद पोटी और गहरे रंग का पेशाब खतरे के निशान हैं, तुरंत जांच कराएं।
Early surgery in biliary atresia can preserve native liver function. — पित्त नली की बीमारी में जल्दी ऑपरेशन से लीवर बचाया जा सकता है।
Good nutrition and vitamin supplementation are important. — अच्छा पोषण और विटामिन की खुराक ज़रूरी है।
Regular follow-up and monitoring are essential. — नियमित जांच बहुत ज़रूरी है।
flow_logic: If jaundice persists beyond 14 days, check stool and urine color
If pale stools or dark urine are present, do LFT with direct bilirubin
If conjugated hyperbilirubinemia is confirmed, suspect neonatal cholestasis
Refer early for imaging and specialist evaluation
quick_checklist_for_opd: Is stool color pale or white?
Has direct / conjugated bilirubin been measured?
Have LFTs, PT/INR and abdominal USG been ordered?
Has early referral been made if biliary atresia is suspected?

standard_prescription_format.title: Standard Prescription - Neonatal Cholestasis
provisional_diagnosis_options: Neonatal cholestasis - rule out biliary atresia
Neonatal cholestasis - likely hepatocellular / metabolic cause

if_biliary_atresia_suspected: Further imaging including HIDA scan if advised
if_unwell_or_coagulopathy_present: Admit for further evaluation and stabilization

warning_signs: Pale or white stools — सफ़ेद या मिट्टी जैसी पोटी
Dark yellow or brown urine — गहरे पीले या भूरे रंग का पेशाब
Bleeding from any site — कहीं से भी खून आना
Poor feeding or weight loss — दूध न पीना या वज़न कम होना
expected_course: Neonatal cholestasis requires urgent evaluation. Biliary atresia must be ruled out within the first 45-60 days of life for best surgical outcomes (Kasai procedure). Metabolic and infectious causes may respond to specific treatment. Without timely intervention, progressive liver damage and cirrhosis can occur.
key_clinical_points: Any jaundice persisting beyond 14 days in a term infant must be fractionated (total + direct bilirubin).
Direct bilirubin >1 mg/dL (if total <5) or >20% of total (if total >5) defines cholestasis.
Stool color card is a simple, effective screening tool for parents.
High GGT (>300 IU/L) with hepatomegaly is suggestive of biliary atresia.
Kasai portoenterostomy should ideally be performed before 45-60 days of age.
Fat-soluble vitamin supplementation (A, D, E, K) is essential in cholestasis.

severity_assessment.mild: Persistent jaundice with normal direct bilirubin, normal stool color, thriving baby — monitor and recheck.
severity_assessment.moderate: Elevated direct bilirubin, slightly abnormal LFTs, normal stool color — complete workup urgently.
severity_assessment.severe: Pale/acholic stools, high GGT, hepatomegaly, coagulopathy, failure to thrive — urgent specialist referral, suspect biliary atresia.

monitoring_parameters.frequency: Weekly until resolved or diagnosis confirmed
Daily by parents using stool color card
